text:
ByAnna Tingley

If you purchase an independently reviewed product or service through a link on our website, Variety may receive an affiliate commission.

After two action-packed weeks, filled withgold medals,bulges,bad breakdancing(and even onerevoked bronze medal for Team USA), theParis 2024Summer Olympics officially comes to a close on Sunday afternoon. The Olympics Closing Ceremony will be held in Paris’s 800,000-seat Stade de France on Sunday, Aug. 11. The two-hour event will stream live onPeacock.

Titled “Records” and hosted indoors on the 2,800 square meter stage of the Stade de France, the closing ceremony promises “new interpretations” and will “take spectators on a journey through time, both past and future,” organizers stated. The show will chart milestones throughout the history of the Olympic Games, which is believed to date back to Ancient Greece.Popular on VarietyRelated Stories‘The Long Walk’ Submits Entire Ensemble in Supporting Acting Categories for Oscars and Other Awards (EXCLUSIVE)British Independent Film Awards: BDSM Romance 'Pillion' Claims Top Honor, 'My Father's Shadow' Wins Best Director

The Olympics Closing Ceremony will take place on Sunday, Aug 11 at 3 p.m. ET. NBC will also air a preshow, highlighting the most standout moments from this year’s Games, at 2 p.m. ET, and an encore taping of the main event during primetime.

The Olympics Closing Ceremony will broadcast on NBC and stream live onPeacock, the official streaming home to the Paris 2024 Summer Olympics. Those without cable can also tune into the ceremony live online through a variety of live TV streamers such asDirecTV,Sling TV,Fubo TVandHulu + Live TV, which all offer free trials.

This year’s Closing Ceremony is set to be one of the most star-studded sendoffs in years.Billie Eilish, Snoop Dogg and the Red Hot Chili Peppersare expected to perform during the Closing Ceremony. All three artists will be seen from Los Angeles in a mix of pre-taped and live performances.

The Grammy, Oscar and Emmy winning H.E.R. will also be in attendance, performing the U.S. national anthem. She’ll be joined by iconic French bands Phoenix and Air, whoare also set to be a part of the ceremony.

Lastly,Tom Cruise is even set to perform a stunt at the Closing Ceremony. The actor, who’s currently shooting “Mission Impossible 8” and attended the opening ceremony in addition to women’s gymnastics and swimming competitions, will hand over the flag to Los Angeles for the 2028 Games.

A Variety and iHeartRadio Podcast

The Business of Entertainment
title: How to Watch the Olympics Closing Ceremony Live Online
author: Anna Tingley
description: The Paris 2024 Summer Olympics will conclude with a closing ceremony on Sunday, Aug. 11. Here's how to watch the Olympics Closing Ceremony live online.
